items.title: Gradient Boosting
items.description: The sequential residual-fitting loop, loss gradients, and learning rate live in Gradient Boosting. This topic covers the production libraries (XGBoost, LightGBM) and their histogram / growth optimizations — not the general boosting derivation.

items.title: Random Forests
items.description: Random Forests bag parallel deep trees with feature subsampling — low bias per tree, variance reduced by averaging. GBDT libraries add trees sequentially to fix errors; RF is the natural bagging baseline to compare before tuning XGBoost or LightGBM.

items.title: Missing Values
items.description: XGBoost and LightGBM route NaN down learned default branches at each split. Missing Values covers imputation, indicator columns, and when explicit preprocessing beats in-tree defaults — compare both on validation metrics.

items.title: Tracking Metrics
items.description: GBDT training runs many boosting rounds — log validation AUC, RMSE, or log-loss per iteration and record best_iteration from early stopping. Tracking Metrics is the canonical home for experiment logging and comparing XGBoost vs LightGBM configs.

items.title: Decision Trees
items.description: Each boosting round adds a shallow decision tree as the weak learner. Split gain, depth limits, and leaf constraints in XGBoost/LightGBM are applied to individual trees inside the ensemble.
